reason: Response B's structure matches my habits on aesthetic-judgment prompts: it opens by flagging the subjectivity of the choice ("temperamental rather than typographic gospel"), steelmans the runner-up (the semicolon) before returning to its pick on a stated criterion (restraint and suggestion), and lands on a crisp earned aphorism rather than a crescendo. Response A commits unequivocally and escalates into uniform, slightly purple grandiosity ending on a moral flourish, which reads more like an imitator reaching for "lyrical Claude" without the metacognitive hedging and self-balancing I tend to include. I'm moderately but not fully confident, since A's measured personification of each mark is also plausibly mine.
